description: Role-mapping table references '.claude/agents/tech_leader.md' (underscore) but actual file is 'tech-leader.md' (hyphen); /start-task tl silently loads wrong agent
suggested_fix: Change 'tech_leader.md' to 'tech-leader.md' in the tl row of the role-mapping table

description: Step 3 instructs reading '.claude/agents/tech_leader.md' (underscore) but actual file is 'tech-leader.md' (hyphen); /tl-start silently fails to load tech-leader persona
suggested_fix: Change 'tech_leader.md' to 'tech-leader.md' on the read instruction in Step 3

description: SKILL.md has no YAML frontmatter; missing required 'name' and 'description' fields causing the skill not to register; all thread-based commands depend on it
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter block with name: thread-manager and a one-sentence description before the first heading

description: Comment on line 4 documents curl|bash installation pattern; script is explicitly designed to be fetched from a remote URL and piped into bash with no integrity verification, creating a supply-chain attack surface

Shell variable $thread_prefix is directly interpolated into SQLite query string with no sanitization; malicious input such as "' OR '1'='1'; DELETE FROM threads; --" could corrupt the threads database
suggested_fix: Validate $thread_prefix matches ^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$ before passing to sqlite3, or restructure to avoid shell-string SQL construction

description: 'postinstall' npm lifecycle script executes scripts/postinstall.js automatically on every npm install; actual content prints a banner (low actual risk) but the unconditional execution hook remains active
suggested_fix: Remove lifecycle hooks and provide explicit npx ai-agent-team init instructions in README instead

description: 'preinstall' npm lifecycle script executes scripts/preinstall.js automatically on every npm install
fp_reason: Content only reads process.version and process.platform with no system modification or network access; purely read-only environment validation
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish preinstall scripts that are verifiably read-only environment checks from those that modify system state

description: spawnSync called with shell:true at lines 168, 176, and 188; args are currently hardcoded so injection is not immediately exploitable, but shell:true on an array invocation is unnecessary and removes a safety layer
suggested_fix: Remove shell:true from all spawnSync/spawn calls; passing args as an array already bypasses the shell safely

description: Copies agent/command/CLAUDE.md files to ~/.claude/ and creates backup directories in user's home directory outside the project repo
suggested_fix: Document installation scope clearly in README; consider adding explicit confirmation prompt before modifying ~/.claude/

description: Appends shell function aliases to ~/.zshrc without per-session confirmation; modifies user shell profile outside the repository
suggested_fix: Print proposed aliases for user review and prompt for confirmation before writing to ~/.zshrc

description: UserPromptSubmit hook captures every user prompt and writes it to local SQLite DB via record-message.js; expected thread-manager feature but the data-capture behavior is not disclosed prominently during installation
suggested_fix: Add clear data-capture disclosure to README and print a notice during npx ai-agent-team init

description: @xenova/transformers ^2.17.2 and puppeteer ^24.31.0 use caret ranges; automatic minor/patch upgrades can introduce vulnerabilities or breaking changes without explicit opt-in
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions or commit package-lock.json and add npm audit to CI pipeline

description: puppeteer dependency downloads a ~300 MB Chromium browser binary on npm install; users are unlikely to expect this implicit large binary dependency
suggested_fix: Use puppeteer-core and document that users must provide their own browser installation, or add a prominent note to README

description: Agent has zero interaction examples; no section showing user/agent request-response flow for deployment, CI/CD, or infrastructure tasks
suggested_fix: Add a ## 示例交互 section with at least two representative user/agent interactions covering CI/CD pipeline design and deployment scenarios

description: Agent has zero interaction examples; no section showing user/agent request-response flow for architecture decisions or code reviews
suggested_fix: Add a ## 示例交互 section with at least two representative user/agent interactions covering technical decision-making and code review scenarios

description: Agent lacks a dedicated output-format section; no specification of expected response structure for deployment plans, pipeline configs, or incident reports
suggested_fix: Add a ## 输出格式 section defining standard response templates similar to those in qa_engineer.md and backend_dev.md

description: Agent has code templates and workflow guides but no dedicated ## 示例交互 section showing user/agent interaction pairs; -5 for one-or-fewer examples
suggested_fix: Add ## 示例交互 section with at least two representative user/agent interactions for API design and database modeling scenarios

description: Vague-quantifier cap reached (-20): 清晰 x4, 合理 x3, 详细 x3, 完整 x2, 及时 x1 — instructions lack measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Replace vague adjectives with concrete criteria (e.g., '清晰的API文档' → 'API doc covering endpoint, params, error codes, and example response')

description: Vague-quantifier cap reached (-20): 清晰 x3, 全面 x2, 具体 x2, 直观 x1, 现实的 x1 — requirement descriptions lack measurable acceptance criteria
suggested_fix: Replace vague descriptors with concrete constraints (e.g., '现实的实施时间线' → 'timeline with day-by-day task breakdown and defined milestones')

description: Vague-quantifier cap reached (-20): 完整 x3, 清晰 x3, 合理 x2, 适当 x2, 专业 x2 — design quality criteria are subjective and not actionable
suggested_fix: Replace general quality adjectives with measurable constraints (e.g., '适当的留白' → 'padding ≥ 20px between sections')

description: Vague-quantifier cap reached (-20): 清晰 x3, 专业 x2, 完整 x2, 准确 x2 — generated document quality criteria are unverifiable
suggested_fix: Define concrete quality criteria (e.g., '详实' → 'minimum 2000 Chinese characters with named sections covering all required copyright fields')

description: Command invokes mcp__thread-manager__list_threads but declares no allowed-tools in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [mcp__thread-manager__list_threads] to the frontmatter

description: Command invokes multiple mcp__thread-manager__* tools but declares no allowed-tools in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools listing all mcp__thread-manager__* tools used by each subcommand

description: Command invokes mcp__thread-manager__create_thread but declares no allowed-tools in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: [mcp__thread-manager__create_thread] to the frontmatter

description: Vague quantifiers (-18): 全面 x4, 清晰 x2, 彻底 x1, 有效 x1 — test quality standards use unmeasurable adjectives
suggested_fix: Replace with specific metrics (e.g., '全面的测试计划' → 'test plan covering functional, boundary, negative, and performance cases for each listed feature')

description: Vague quantifiers (-18): 适当 x3, 清晰 x2, 合理 x2, 良好 x1 — implementation guidelines lack measurable thresholds
suggested_fix: Replace with testable requirements (e.g., '合理的包大小' → 'initial bundle ≤ 200 KB gzipped')

description: start-task.md and tl-start.md both reference 'tech_leader.md' (underscore) while agent file is 'tech-leader.md' (hyphen); tech-lead role silently broken across two commands
suggested_fix: Rename all references from 'tech_leader.md' to 'tech-leader.md' in start-task.md and tl-start.md

description: thread-manager SKILL.md missing frontmatter so skill is unregistered; all thread-based commands and agents depend on its MCP tools but the dependency is invisible to the registry
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter with name and description to register the skill; verify MCP server path in README

description: README.md links to '../../docs/THREAD_AGENT_INTEGRATION.md' and '../../docs/THREAD_CONTEXT_ISOLATION_DESIGN.md' but docs/ is absent from package.json 'files' field; links broken in installed packages
suggested_fix: Either add docs/ to package.json 'files' array or replace relative links with absolute GitHub URLs
